verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein expressed exclusively in sperm cells. In mature spermatozoa it resides on the inner acrosomal membrane and is relocated to the entire sperm plasma membrane surface upon acrosome reaction (acrosomal exocytosis), where it becomes exposed on the extracellular face. This surface exposure is essential for sperm-egg fusion via interaction with JUNO on the egg. Because surface display is restricted to the narrow germline/gamete lineage and requires a specific activation event (acrosome reaction), the verdict is contextual / tissue_restricted_surface.
